items.headline: Two new single-family homes permitted on Chamberlain Drive
items.summary: Building permits issued for two new 2-story houses at 3550 and 3554 Chamberlain Dr, representing new housing supply in the area. Combined project value is approximately $1.09 million.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank signage permitted at 250 Lytton Avenue
items.summary: Permit issued to install three illuminated LED wall signs for Fifth Third Bank at a downtown Palo Alto commercial building. The bank's expansion into this location represents a new financial services tenant in the area.

items.headline: Sunnyvale studying ban on chemical pesticides on city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review a policy to eliminate the use of chemical pesticides on all city-owned or leased property. This would affect parks, open spaces, and facilities managed by the city.

items.headline: Sustainability Commission to review artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is studying the city's use of artificial turf, likely examining environmental and maintenance implications for parks and recreational areas.

items.headline: Planning Commission to hear rezoning of Homestead Road property with two homes
items.summary: The Planning Commission will hold a public hearing on a proposal to remove historic district protections from 1341 Homestead Road to allow it to be subdivided into two separate parcels, each containing one existing residence. The property at 906 Monroe Street would retain its historic district designation. The rezoning requires consistency review with the Downtown Precise Plan.

items.headline: Library parcel tax rates to increase for FY 2026-27
items.summary: San José City Council voted to increase parcel tax rates that fund the public library system for the upcoming fiscal year. Residents pay this tax on their property bills.

items.headline: Eight homes proposed on single-family lot in Sunnyvale
items.summary: A developer proposes demolishing one house at 781 S Wolfe Road and building eight two-story homes on the 0.69-acre site, subdividing it into nine lots. The project would have a 65.8% floor area ratio and requires special development permit approval.
